description: Carries only absolute or same-document OBI-defined references: every sources[*].location is an absolute URI or a bindingSpec-defined absolute address (never a relative reference); every schema $ref is a same-document fragment in JSON Pointer form (plain-name $anchor fragments are not used at OBI positions) or an absolute URI; every schema $id at an OBI position is absolute (a nested $id within a schema declaring its own $id resolves against that resource's base per 2020-12 and is resource-internal); every named-transform $ref in bindings[*].inputTransform/outputTransform is a same-document fragment. $dynamicRef and $dynamicAnchor do not appear at OBI positions (dynamic resolution follows the runtime dynamic scope rather than the document); within a schema declaring its own $id both are that resource's internal business, per the same scope rule as $ref/$anchor. URI-form references are well-formed per RFC 3986 §4.1; a bindingSpec-defined absolute address (e.g. a gRPC host:port) is well-formed per its own binding specification and is exempt. bindings[*].ref is interpreted by the governing binding specification and is out of scope for this rule.
notes: Format-defined absolute addresses (e.g. a gRPC host:port) are exempt from RFC 3986 URI well-formedness; their validity is per-format and cannot be checked by a format-agnostic validator, so positive cases for them are not fixtured here (same per-format testability limitation as OBI-D-13 and OBI-T-06). The relative-reference clause is format-agnostically decidable per the rule's verification note: a location with no ':' before its first '/', '?', or '#' is a relative reference in form (RFC 3986 section 4.2) and every validator rejects it -- including a bare name like example.com; only colon-bearing non-URI strings are per-format. The relative-reference and malformed-URI negatives below apply to the URI-form branch.

tests.description: bare name in source location (no colon before the first separator: relative-reference form, rejectable without format knowledge)
relative URI reference in source location
absolute URI containing disallowed angle-bracket characters in source location
URI containing a backtick in schema $ref
plain-name ($anchor) fragment $ref is not JSON Pointer form
plain-name fragment inside an embedded $id-declaring schema is resource-internal (out of D-05's scope)
$dynamicRef at an OBI position (dynamic resolution follows the runtime dynamic scope, not the document)
$dynamicAnchor at an OBI position (a second named-schema mechanism competing with the schemas map, as $anchor would be)
dynamic pair inside an embedded $id-declaring schema is resource-internal (out of D-05's scope; full 2020-12 semantics, e.g. recursive extension)
a nested relative $id inside an embedded $id-declaring schema is resource-internal (resolves against the resource base per 2020-12; out of D-05's scope)
a property NAMED $dynamicRef under properties is data, not a keyword (keyword-position guard)
